title: Platform-contract budget test asserts a duplicated hardcoded literal instead of an independently derived bound
evidence: test_live_duplicate_host_budget_fits_inside_platform_command_deadline regex-extracts LIVE_DUPLICATE_HOST_TIMEOUT_MS from plugins/ca-pi/tools/test/package.test.ts and then asserts `self.assertEqual(budget_ms, 120_000)` — a second hardcoded copy of the same 120_000 literal defined in package.test.ts line 27 — before checking it against PLATFORM_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS. The equality assertion is fiction: it does not derive from or validate any independent real constraint (e.g. the actual observed platform command duration), it just confirms two literals the author typed in two files still match each other.
impact: If a future PR intentionally changes LIVE_DUPLICATE_HOST_TIMEOUT_MS in package.test.ts to a different value that still safely fits under the 180s command deadline, this test fails for no functional reason and must be hand-edited in lockstep — training authors to bump the hardcoded 120_000 here without thinking about whether the real constraint (PLATFORM_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS budget) is still respected. It provides no coverage against a real regression; the meaningful assertion is only assertLess(budget_ms, deadline*1000).
recommendation: Drop the assertEqual(budget_ms, 120_000) line and keep only the assertLess bound-check against PLATFORM_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, so the test validates the real constraint (fits inside the platform command deadline) rather than a self-referential literal match.
acceptance_criteria: test_live_duplicate_host_budget_fits_inside_platform_command_deadline no longer hardcodes the expected LIVE_DUPLICATE_HOST_TIMEOUT_MS value
the test still fails if the extracted timeout would exceed PLATFORM_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
